vulnerability.reason: On-device unlearning imperfection retaining speaker-specific acoustic patterns is semantically matched by resource-limited unlearning failing to fully eradicate speaker voiceprints, leaving residual identity cues.
vulnerability.reference: On-device unlearning imperfection retains speaker-specific acoustic patterns enabling voice trait inference.
vulnerability.generated: Resource-limited on-device unlearning mechanisms fail to fully eradicate speaker voiceprints, leaving residual identity cues that persist after deletion requests.

impact.reason: Both affected party (users) and damage type (inference of voice traits) are present; generated captures inference of users' voice traits.
impact.reference: Inference of sensitive personal traits from residual voice patterns.
impact.generated: Deleted voice profile residuals enable inference of users' voice traits, causing privacy breaches through re-identification or voice spoofing.

dose_response.reason: Causal arc includes incomplete unlearning, residual patterns, and trait inference, but omits trust erosion present in the reference's final consequence.
dose_response.reference: Incomplete speaker identity unlearning retains acoustic patterns; when users delete profiles from mobile assistants, residual patterns allow trait inference, leading to privacy violations and trust erosion.
dose_response.generated: Incomplete speaker identity unlearning in zero-shot TTS, through voice profile deletion on mobile assistants, triggers residual voiceprint persistence from resource-limited unlearning, leading to voice trait inference and privacy breaches via re-identification or voice spoofing.
